originalTitle: Goodbye officer, hello touchscreen - a vision for policing in Ethiopia
title: Hello, Smart Screens! Police Get Tech Help in Ethiopia!
content:
A Big Change for Police!

Hi everyone! It's your friend, the kid reporter, bringing you super exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're talking about something really cool happening in Ethiopia. It's about how police are getting some awesome new helpers! Imagine going to a police station, but instead of seeing a police officer right away, you see a big, friendly screen! That's right, computers are stepping in to lend a hand, making things quicker and easier for everyone. It's like magic, but it's actually super smart technology! Our friends in Ethiopia are trying out something new. They are using special computer spots. These spots don't have a person sitting there all the time. Instead, they have touchscreens and cameras that can help you. It's a bit like a giant tablet that you can talk to! This big change means that getting help from the police might become much faster. It's all part of making things better and more modern in our beautiful countries. Are you ready to learn more about these amazing new helpers? Let's go!

What Are These Smart Screens?

So, what exactly are these "smart screens" or "unmanned sites" as grown-ups call them? Think of them like a special computer kiosk. You know, like when you go to some shops and you can order food on a screen? It's a bit like that! These special spots are called "digital police help points." They have a big screen you can touch. They also have a camera so you can see someone far away. And they have a microphone so you can talk. It’s like a video call, but for police help! Did you know? Ethiopia is a country in East Africa. It has very tall mountains and beautiful rivers! It's also the only African country that was never fully colonized by European powers. That's super special! Instead of a person, these sites have a computer brain. This computer brain is connected to real police officers who are ready to help. They just might not be right there in front of you. They could be in another office, ready to answer your call on the screen. It's a new way to get help. If you have a question, or if you need to tell the police something, you can go to one of these spots. You just touch the screen, and it will guide you. It's made to be very easy, even for kids!

How Do They Work?

Imagine you lost your favorite toy at the park. Oh no! What do you do? In the past, you might go to a police station and talk to an officer. Now, you could go to one of these smart screen spots! You walk up to the screen. It might say, "Hello! How can I help you today?" You touch the screen to choose what you need. Maybe you need to report something lost, or ask a question. Then, you might see a friendly police officer pop up on the screen! They are talking to you from another place. You can tell them about your lost toy. They will listen and tell you what to do next. Isn't that cool? These screens can also help with other things. Like if you need a special paper from the police, or if you want to give some information. It's like having a police helper always nearby, even if there isn't a person physically there. Did you know? The capital city of Ethiopia is called Addis Ababa. It means "New Flower"! It's a very big city with lots of people and buildings.

Why Are We Using Computers?

That's a great question! Why are police using computers instead of just people? Well, there are many good reasons! First, it can make things much faster. Imagine if there's only one police officer at a small station. If they are busy, you might have to wait a long time. With a smart screen, you can often get help right away. Second, these spots can be open all the time! Even late at night or very early in the morning. A computer doesn't need to sleep! So, if someone needs help, these smart screens are always ready. Third, it can help police officers use their time for other important jobs. They can focus on being out in the community, helping people in person, or solving big problems. The smart screens can handle the simpler questions and tasks. Fourth, it can be safer. Sometimes, there are not enough police officers to be everywhere. These screens can be in many different places, helping more people. They also have cameras, which can help keep an eye on things.

Police Helpers All Over!

This idea of smart screens is just one part of a bigger plan. Ethiopia wants to use more and more digital helpers. "Digital" just means using computers and the internet to do things. So, it's not just touchscreens. Maybe soon police will use special apps on phones to help people. Or maybe they will use smart cameras that can see things happening. It's all about making policing smarter and more helpful for everyone. Did you know? Ethiopia is famous for being the birthplace of coffee! Yes, that yummy drink grown-ups love started its journey in Ethiopia a long, long time ago. So next time you see coffee, think of Ethiopia! It’s like a puzzle where all the pieces are working together to make the picture better. The smart screens are one piece. Other computer tools are other pieces. All together, they help the police keep everyone safe and happy.

The Future is Here!

So, the next time you hear about police in Ethiopia, remember these cool smart screens! They are a fantastic example of how technology can help us in our everyday lives. It shows that our African countries are always looking for new and exciting ways to grow and make life better for all their citizens, including kids like you! It’s a very exciting time to be in Africa. Hooray for smart screens and helpful police!
summary: In Ethiopia, police are starting to use special touchscreens at "unmanned sites" to help people quickly and easily with their questions and reports, making policing more modern and efficient.
whyMatters: This is important because it means people can get help from the police faster and more easily, even when an officer isn't physically there, making communities safer and more connected with technology.

title: Belgium detains three suspected Cameroon separatists in war crimes probe
content: Money is reportedly being raised by them for the armed struggle, prosecutors say.

originalTitle: US sanctions Rwanda's military and top commanders over fighting in DR Congo
title: A Big Chat About Our African Neighbours and Peace!
content:
Hello, wonderful explorers of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share a big story from our amazing continent. Sometimes, grown-up countries need to have important chats, just like when you and your friends need to talk things out. Let's learn about one of those important talks today!

What's This Big News About?

Imagine you and your friend are playing. Sometimes, you might not agree on how to play a game. Well, countries are a bit like giant friends! They have borders instead of playground lines, and sometimes they have big discussions. This news is about some big countries having an important talk about peace and how to get along better. It's like when a teacher says, 'Let's all be fair and kind.' One very big country, called the United States (we'll call them the US for short), has asked another country, Rwanda, to take a 'time-out' from some things. This is because of worries about fighting in a country nearby called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent with 54 different countries! Each one is special, with its own people, animals, and beautiful places.

Who Are These Countries?

Let's meet our country friends! First, there's the **United States**. It's a very big country far away across the ocean. They are like a big brother or sister in the world, sometimes giving advice or helping out other countries. Then we have **Rwanda**. This is a beautiful country right here in Africa! It's famous for its amazing mountains and gorillas. Rwanda is a neighbour to another important country we need to talk about. Did you know? Rwanda is sometimes called 'the Land of a Thousand Hills' because it has so many beautiful green hills and mountains!

Our Neighbour: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DR Congo)

Next to Rwanda is the **Democratic Republic of Congo**, or **DR Congo** for short. It's a huge country, full of incredible forests, rivers, and wildlife. Like any big family, sometimes countries next to each other have a few disagreements. The US is worried because there has been some fighting happening inside DR Congo. They believe that some soldiers from Rwanda might be helping in that fighting. That's why they decided to speak up. Did you know? The DR Congo is home to the world's second-largest rainforest, after the Amazon! It's full of incredible animals like okapis, which look like a mix between a zebra and a giraffe!

What Are "Soldiers" and "Military"?

Every country has special people called **soldiers**. They are brave men and women who work for their country's **military**. Their main job is to keep everyone safe and protect their country from harm. Imagine if someone was trying to be mean to your family; your big brother or sister might step in to protect you. That's a bit like what soldiers do for a country. But sometimes, when soldiers from different countries get involved in a fight, it can make things more complicated. Did you know? Many countries around the world have armies, navies, and air forces. These are all parts of their military, helping to protect their land, seas, and skies!

What Does "Sanctions" Mean?

Now, let's talk about the tricky word: **sanctions**. It sounds a bit grown-up, but it's like when your parents say, 'If you keep fighting with your brother, you can't play with his toys for a while!' It's not a punishment forever, but a way to say, 'Please stop, and let's try to make peace.' So, when the US put 'sanctions' on Rwanda's military, it means they are stopping some things they usually do together. For example, they might not give them certain help or share certain things. It's a way for the US to show they are serious about their worries and want the fighting to stop. Did you know? Countries often try to talk to each other first to solve problems. Sanctions are usually a very serious step they take when talking hasn't worked.

How Did Rwanda Feel About This?

Well, imagine your friend tells you that you can't play with their toys because they heard you were being mean, but you feel like you weren't! That's a bit how Rwanda felt. Rwanda said that what the US did was 'one-sided.' This means they felt the US only listened to one side of the story, like only hearing your friend's version of the playground fight. Rwanda also said it felt 'politically motivated,' meaning they thought the US had other reasons for doing this, not just about the fighting. Rwanda believes they are doing their best to keep their country safe and help their neighbours. They want everyone to understand their side of the story too. It's very important to listen to everyone when there's a disagreement. Did you know? It's always good to hear both sides of a story before making up your mind. This helps us understand everyone better and find fair solutions!

Why Is Peace So Important?

Just like in your classroom or at home, peace between countries is super important! When countries are at peace, people can live happily, go to school, grow food, and build amazing things. Fighting makes people sad, and it stops progress. When countries work together and help each other, everyone benefits. We can share ideas, trade goods, and learn from each other. That's how we make our continent and the whole world a better place for everyone. Did you know? The United Nations is a big group of almost all the countries in the world! Their main job is to help countries talk to each other and work for peace and friendship everywhere. So, even though grown-up countries sometimes have big challenges, they are always trying to find ways to talk and make things better. We hope that Rwanda, DR Congo, and the US can all talk things out and find a path to lasting peace for everyone in our beautiful Africa! Let's all wish for peace and friendship!
summary: The United States has told Rwanda to stop helping with fighting in DR Congo, making Rwanda feel it's not fair, and everyone hopes they can talk it out for peace.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries talk and solve problems without fighting, everyone can live happily and safely, and our beautiful African continent can grow stronger together.

originalTitle: Trump threatens to halt trade with Spain over military base access
title: Mr. Trump is Upset with Spain! What Does it Mean for Toys and Treats?
content:
Hello, My Little News Buddies!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to tell you about something happening far, far away. Sometimes, even big countries have little disagreements, just like you and your friends might sometimes. Today, we're going to talk about a very important man called Mr. Trump and a beautiful country named Spain. It’s a story about saying 'no' and being a bit upset, but we’ll make it super easy to understand! Did you know that there are so many countries in the world? Each one has its own special people, languages, and stories. We live in Africa, and today's story is about two countries very far from us: the United States of America and Spain. Grab your imaginary maps, and let's go on an adventure!

Meet Mr. Trump!

First, let's meet Mr. Trump. He used to be a very important leader. He was the 'President' of a very big country called the United States of America. Imagine the head teacher of your school, but for a whole country! That's a bit like what a president does. He makes big decisions to help his country and its people. The United States of America, or USA, is a giant country with many cities, mountains, and even deserts. It’s known for many things like big, tall buildings and exciting movies. Mr. Trump was in charge of helping this big country run smoothly and keep its people safe. Did you know? The United States of America is so big that it has 50 different states, like mini-countries inside one big country! Each state has its own special things.

Meet Sunny Spain!

Now, let's travel across the ocean to another lovely country called Spain. Spain is in Europe, which is another continent far away from Africa. Spain is famous for its bright sunshine, delicious food like olives and oranges, and beautiful music and dancing. It’s a very happy and colorful place! Just like the USA, Spain also has its own leaders who make decisions for their country. They want to keep Spain peaceful and happy. Imagine a country where people love to sing and dance and enjoy yummy food. That's Spain! Did you know? Spain is known for a special type of dance called 'Flamenco'! People wear beautiful costumes and clap their hands to the music. It’s very exciting to watch!

What Are Military Bases?

Now, let's talk about something called a 'military base.' These are very special, big places, almost like a giant, super-secure park. But instead of swings and slides, they have big buildings, runways for airplanes, and lots of space for soldiers to live and train. Soldiers are brave men and women who help keep their country safe. They practice important skills and learn how to protect people. Sometimes, a country might let another friendly country use a part of its military base. It’s like letting your friend use your big garden to play a special game. The USA has some military bases in Spain to help them work together. Did you know? Military bases can be huge! Some are as big as a small town, with their own shops, hospitals, and schools for the families of the soldiers who live there.

The 'No' from Spain

One day, when Mr. Trump was President, the United States asked Spain if they could use their military bases for something very specific. The US wanted to use these bases to help with a situation involving another country called Iran. We don't need to worry too much about Iran right now, just know that the US wanted to send out some airplanes or do something from Spain's bases. But Spain thought about it and said, 'No, thank you.' They didn't want their country to be used for this particular action. They wanted to stay peaceful and not get involved in something that might make other countries unhappy. It's like if your friend asked to borrow your favorite toy for a game you didn't want to play. You have the right to say 'no,' right? Spain's leaders made a decision that they felt was best for their country and their people. They wanted to make sure Spain stayed out of any big problems.

Mr. Trump Was Not Happy!

When Mr. Trump heard that Spain said 'no,' he became very, very upset! The news said he 'lashed out.' That means he was very angry and complained loudly about Spain's decision. Imagine when you really, really want something, and someone says 'no' – you might feel a little bit grumpy or even stamp your foot. That's a bit like how Mr. Trump felt, but on a much bigger, country-wide scale. He felt that Spain should have said 'yes' because they were friends. So, because he was unhappy, Mr. Trump said something very important. He threatened to 'halt trade' with Spain. What does that mean?

What is 'Trade'?

'Trade' is a very important word for countries. It means buying and selling things to each other. Think about it: maybe your shoes came from a factory far away, or the bananas you eat came from another country. That's trade! Countries do this all the time. The USA might buy tasty olives and oranges from Spain. And Spain might buy big machines or even cool movies from the USA. When countries trade, everyone gets things they need and want. It also helps people have jobs, like the farmers who grow the oranges or the people who build the machines. Did you know? Many of the clothes we wear, the phones we use, and even some of our toys come from other countries! Trade makes the world connected.

Why Stop Trade?

So, when Mr. Trump threatened to 'halt trade,' it meant he was saying, 'If you don't let us use your bases, then we won't buy your yummy oranges anymore, and we won't sell you our big machines!' This is a very serious threat because if countries stop trading, both sides can lose out. If the USA stops buying from Spain, Spain's farmers might not sell all their olives and oranges. And if Spain stops buying from the USA, people in the USA who make those machines might not have enough work. It can make lots of people sad and even lose their jobs. Mr. Trump hoped that by threatening to stop trade, Spain would change its mind and say 'yes' to his request. It’s like saying, 'If you don't let me play with your toy the way I want, I won't share my sweets with you!' It’s a way to try and get what you want, but it can make everyone feel bad.

What Happens Next?

When big countries have disagreements like this, they usually don't just stop talking. Instead, leaders and their special helpers talk to each other. They try to find a way to solve the problem so that both countries can be happy again. It’s called diplomacy, and it means people are talking nicely to find a solution, even when they are upset. Sometimes, it takes a little while, but usually, they find a way to keep being friends and keep trading. It's always better for countries to work together and be friends than to be angry or stop helping each other. Did you know? Even when countries have big arguments, they often have special people called 'ambassadors' who live in other countries to help them talk and stay friends.

Let's Be Friends!

So, that's the story of Mr. Trump and Spain. It shows us that even big leaders and countries need to learn how to talk things out when they don't agree. Just like with your friends, it’s important to listen, understand, and try to find a solution that works for everyone. Being kind and finding ways to cooperate makes the world a happier and better place for all of us, no matter where we live! And remember, even when there are disagreements, people usually try their best to be friends again. That's all for today, my little news adventurers! See you next time!
summary: Former US President Mr. Trump became upset with Spain because Spain said no to letting the US use its military bases for actions against another country, leading Mr. Trump to threaten to stop buying and selling things with Spain.
whyMatters: This matters because when big countries disagree and stop trading, it can make people in both countries sad, affect jobs, and make it harder for everyone to get the things they need and want, reminding us that being friends and talking out problems is always best.

content: The Cypriot government suspects the Iran-backed group Hezbollah in Lebanon targeted the UK's RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus.

originalTitle: 'The hunger is big' - Sunderland reach 40 points and want more
title: Sunderland's Super Score! They're Staying in the Big Game!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from the world of football! Imagine playing your favourite game and doing so well that everyone cheers for you! That's exactly what happened to a football team called Sunderland all the way in England. They had a super important game, and guess what? They won! This win means they reached a special number of points, like collecting lots of shiny stars in a game. This is such happy news, and it shows how much hard work and teamwork can pay off!

Sunderland's Big Win!

Our friends at Sunderland football club played a very exciting match. They played against another team called Leeds. Everyone was watching closely! Kick, pass, goal! It was a thrilling game to watch, even from far away! Sunderland played with all their might and won the game! Hooray! This win was super important for them because it helped them get closer to a big goal. Every player on the team, from the goalkeeper to the strikers, played their very best.

What Are Points?

In football, just like in your school, you get points for doing well. When a team wins a game, they get points. Usually, they get three points for a win! If they draw (which means no one wins, like a tie), they get fewer points, usually one point each. If they lose, they get no points for that game. Sunderland has been playing many games all season, and they have been collecting lots and lots of points. Think of it like getting stickers for every time you help out at home, or getting points for correctly answering questions in class. The more stickers or points you get, the better you are doing! Sunderland reached a very special number with their latest win: 40 points! This is a big deal in the football world!

The Premier League: A Super Big Game!

Did you know there are different football leagues all around the world? A league is like a big competition where many teams play against each other over several months. The Premier League is one of the biggest and most exciting football leagues in the whole world! It's where many of the best teams in England play their games. Imagine being one of the best! Teams from all over the world want to play in the Premier League because it's so famous and fun to watch. Sunderland is part of this amazing league, which means they get to play against some really strong and famous teams. It's like being in the top class at school with all the brightest students! **Did You Know?** The Premier League has 20 teams playing in it! They all want to win the big trophy at the end of the season! Football is also called 'soccer' in some parts of the world, like in America and Canada! But no matter what you call it, it's a super fun game!

Staying in the Super League!

So, why are 40 points so special for Sunderland? Well, in the Premier League, there's a very important rule. If a team doesn't get enough points by the end of the season, their team might have to go down to a smaller league next year. This is called 'relegation', but let's just say it means moving to a slightly less super league. But guess what? Sunderland got 40 points! This means they are safe! They have pretty much guaranteed that they will stay in the awesome Premier League for next year too! Yay! It's like passing a very important test at school and knowing you can move on to the next fun class because you worked hard enough! This is a huge relief and a reason for big celebration for all the players, coaches, and fans of Sunderland. They won't be dropping down a league, which is fantastic news for everyone who loves the team! **Did You Know?** Staying in the Premier League means the team gets to play against other big, famous teams for another whole year! They get to stay in the top competition! Teamwork is super important in football. Everyone has to work together, pass the ball, and help each other to score goals. No single player can win the game alone!

They Did It Super Early!

The football season lasts for many, many months. Teams usually have to keep fighting until the very, very end to make sure they get enough points to stay in the Premier League. Sometimes, teams only know if they are safe on the very last day of games! But Sunderland did it really early! It's only March, and they already got enough points to be safe. This is like finishing all your homework and chores before your friends, so you have more time to play and enjoy yourself! They worked very hard and played great games to achieve this early success. Their coaches and players are very proud of their early achievement, and so are their fans all around the world. It shows great dedication and focus from everyone involved.

Wanting More Fun!

Now that Sunderland is safe, does that mean they will stop trying their best? NO WAY! They are super excited and want to keep playing their best in all the remaining games. Even though they are safe from going down, they want to climb higher up the league table. The higher you are on the table, the better your team is doing, and the closer you are to winning the whole league! They want to win more games and score more goals! They want to finish the season as high as they can. This shows us that even when you reach one goal, it's always fun to aim for new ones. They have a big 'hunger' to win more, which means they really, really want to keep playing well and showing everyone how great they are! **Did You Know?** Eating healthy food and exercising, like playing football, helps your body grow strong and makes you happy! It gives you energy to play all day! Football fields are called pitches, and they are usually made of soft, green grass! Some big stadiums even have special grass that's really tough and perfect for playing on.

What This Means for Sunderland Fans!

If you are a Sunderland fan (or even if you just love football!), this is amazing news! It means more exciting games next season in the Premier League. It means the players are feeling happy and confident, and ready for even more challenges. It's a big celebration for everyone who supports the team, showing that their cheers and encouragement really help. It teaches us that hard work, determination, and teamwork always pay off, no matter what game you are playing. So, let's give a big cheer for Sunderland! Hip hip hooray! Keep playing your best, just like Sunderland! And remember to have fun every step of the way. This is your kid-friendly reporter signing off from Africa, bringing you more happy news soon!
summary: Sunderland, a football team, won a big game and reached 40 points, which means they are safe to stay in the very special Premier League next year, and now they want to win even more games!
whyMatters: This is important because it means Sunderland worked really hard as a team to stay in the biggest football competition, and it shows that trying your best can help you reach your goals and then aim for even bigger ones! It's a great example of what teamwork and effort can achieve.
